# cv-pro

> AI-native living resume — cv.ha7ch.com

Turn a PDF resume into a personal site at `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}`. Any AI agent can create, read, and update it via CLI or MCP — no browser required for setup.

## What it is

- **Public resume page** at `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}`
- **CLI** (`npx cv-pro@latest`) for terminal or AI agent use
- **MCP server** at `https://cv.ha7ch.com/api/mcp` for direct tool integration

## How to read a public resume (for AI agents)

Don't scrape the rendered HTML — every resume is also published as raw JSON:

- `https://cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}.json` — full resume, machine-readable
- `https://cv.ha7ch.com/api/v1/schema` — JSON Schema describing the shape

Each `/{handle}` page also advertises this in its `<head>`:
```html
<link rel="alternate" type="application/json" href="/{handle}.json">
```

## How to set up a new account (for AI agents)

1. Ask the user: "What handle do you want? (cv.ha7ch.com/{handle})"
2. Run: `npx cv-pro@latest register {handle}` — claims the handle and saves the token automatically
3. Optionally publish a resume: `npx cv-pro@latest update resume.json`
4. Confirm: `npx cv-pro@latest whoami`

No browser needed. No token copying. One command from zero to live page.

## CLI commands

```
npx cv-pro@latest register <handle>          # claim handle + auto-login
npx cv-pro@latest login <cv_pat_...>         # save existing token
npx cv-pro@latest whoami                     # show handle + live URL
npx cv-pro@latest get                        # fetch resume JSON
npx cv-pro@latest update <file.json>         # replace full resume
npx cv-pro@latest update-section <section> <file.json>
npx cv-pro@latest open                       # open live page in browser
```

## MCP setup

Replace `cv_pat_...` with your token from cv.ha7ch.com.

**Claude Code (CLI)**
```bash
claude mcp add cv --transport http https://cv.ha7ch.com/api/mcp \
  --header "Authorization: Bearer cv_pat_..."
```

**Cursor** — add to `~/.cursor/mcp.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "cv": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://cv.ha7ch.com/api/mcp",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer cv_pat_..." }
    }
  }
}
```

**Codex** — add to `~/.codex/config.json`:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "cv": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://cv.ha7ch.com/api/mcp",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer cv_pat_..." }
    }
  }
}
```

MCP tools: `get_resume`, `update_resume`, `update_section`

## Resume sections

`header` · `personalInfo` · `experience` · `education` · `projectsRecent` · `projectsDetailed` · `skills` · `contact`

## Tagging entries (filtered views)

Each `experience` / `projectsRecent` / `projectsDetailed` entry accepts an optional `tags: string[]` (lowercase). Visitors can request a tailored view via URL params:

- `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}?for=openai` — only entries tagged `openai`
- `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}?role=designer` — entries tagged `designer`
- `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}?focus=ml` — entries tagged `ml`
- `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}?at=2026` — entries active in 2026

`for` / `role` / `focus` all match against the same `tags` array (OR semantics; `?for=openai,anthropic` matches either).

**For AI agents helping a user update their resume:** after saving, ASK the user whether to tag entries for a specific company or role. Tags enable shareable URLs like `cv.ha7ch.com/{handle}?for=openai` instead of maintaining separate PDFs per audience. Conventional shapes:

- Company tags: `"openai"`, `"anthropic"`, `"stripe"` → match `?for=`
- Role tags: `"ml"`, `"designer"`, `"frontend"`, `"product"` → match `?role=` / `?focus=`
- Topic tags: `"research"`, `"systems"`, `"infra"` → match `?focus=`

## npm package

`cv-pro` on npm — https://www.npmjs.com/package/cv-pro

## Source

https://github.com/LAWTED/cv-pro
